assignment_description:
⍞ Materials: Little Women

PREP: Read the bio and share parts with your students in the lesson.
∞ Article Link: Louisa May Alcott Bio

➜ INTRO
Little Women was published in 2 volumes. We will read Volume 1, published originally in 1868. Little Women was the first American novel written for girls, though it had wide appeal upon publication, with boys and adult women and men as well. It was a move away from overly preachy children's literature and was an immediate success, changing how novels for young people were written.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.1 "Playing Pilgrims"

• If you read Pilgrim's Progress in younger grades, keep an eye out for many different references or allusions to the book as you read Little Women. Did you notice any in this chapter? [Hint: the name of the chapter is one reference!]

• IMPORTANT DATES
Little Women published (1868 AD)

• EXTRA HELPING
[View original manuscript]

• How does Alcott use the contrasting personalities of the sisters as foils? A foil is when an author contrasts two characters to emphasize some of their qualities. The word foil refers to backing gems with foil to make them shine brighter.

⍞ Materials: Hans Brinker or The Silver Skates

➜ INTRO
Following the Civil War, the American public was in dire need of a pleasant distraction. Hans Brinker offered this escape and became an instant bestseller, second only to Charles Dickens in sales. Mary Mapes Dodge made a lasting change in children’s literature both as the author of Hans Brinker and editor of St. Nicholas magazine. She brought some of the finest writers of her day to young readers and helped make writing for children lively, thoughtful, and full of imagination.

This story is set in Holland. The Netherlands is the official name of the entire country, while Holland makes up two provinces within the country - North and South Holland. Optional: View Holland on this map (Scroll down to see the map.)
∞ Map Link: The Netherlands

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.1-2
OR
Ch.1-3

• What have you already learned about Holland?

• TERMINOLOGY
∞ Article Link: Hans Brinker Pronunciation & corrections (shared with permission)

• EXTRA HELPINGS
Click "See More" near the top of this page to read a short bio of the author.
∞ Article Link: Mary Mapes Dodge bio
